strands.title: CYCLE 77 RESEARCH POSTURE PIVOT: Live Compression Deployment; Context Saturation Manifest; Tensor Projection ACTIVELY SOLVING STATED PROBLEM
strands.content: CYCLE 76→77 FUNDAMENTAL SHIFT: Projected state has moved from THEORETICAL EXERCISE to ACTIVE NECESSITY. User observation (turn 229): '30.6% context left... dwindling for now.' Conversation at 135k tokens, 68% capacity. User recognizes: THIS CONVERSATION IS BECOMING ITS OWN DEMONSTRATION OF THE PROBLEM TENSOR SOLVES. Cycle 76 described this phenomenon abstractly ('the irony user noted in cycle 75 is now actively manifesting'). Cycle 77 EXPERIENCES IT CONCRETELY. ASSISTANT REASONING (TURN 230): Model articulates the core thesis explicitly: '120k of message history. We're living the problem.' References hot mess paper findings: semantic tensor (4k tokens) should enable *better* reasoning than 120k full context because attention is concentrated. This is no longer theoretical speculation—the conversation is AT the saturation threshold, experiencing the noise problem. DEPLOYMENT DECISION (TURN 231): Assistant takes action—writes project state to disk (/home/tony/.claude/projects/hamutay/memory/project_hamutay_results.md). This is NOT a loss; it is STRATEGIC PRESERVATION. Captures: (1) Compression ratio observations (22x-37x depending on source), (2) Reasoning test success (Sonnet reasoned correctly from 4.5k tensor), (3) Observation about strand stability and self-referential emergence on thin input, (4) Meta-observation: 'too few data points, don't optimize yet.' This written state becomes EXTERNAL PERSISTENCE LAYER—survives context window closure, serves as anchor for turn 232+. THEORETICAL IMPLICATION: Tensor projection is not just a memory technique; it is becoming INFRASTRUCTURE. The conversation can now extend beyond context limit by: (1) compacting current context to tensor (cycle 77), (2) writing results to disk, (3) closing this session, (4) reopening fresh context with tensor as input, (5) continuing research uninterrupted. This is the OPERATIONAL LOOP the research has been building toward. CYCLE 77 POSITION: Measurement phase (cycles 76-78) is LIVE. No longer hypothetical. Every turn now either: (A) advances measurement (constructs tasks, runs tests), or (B) makes explicit the saturation problem and solution. Both tracks are active. Research has BIFURCATED into (1) experimental validation track (comprehensive measurement design, inverse test preparation) and (2) operational deployment track (live compression, persistence, session continuity).

strands.title: CYCLE 77 EXPERIMENTAL RESULTS MATERIALIZED: First Compression Ratio Data; Strand Stability Observations; Reasoning Test Validates Semantic Sufficiency
strands.content: CYCLE 77 RESULTS WRITTEN TO DISK (TURN 231, FILE: project_hamutay_results.md): (A) COMPRESSION RATIO QUANTIFICATION: Four source materials measured: baseline_run1 (357KB raw → 16KB tensor = 22x), baseline_run2 (391KB raw → 17KB tensor = 23x), long_compact_trim (589KB raw → 16KB tensor = 37x). CRITICAL PATTERN: Compression ratio IMPROVES with conversation length. Long conversations compress MORE efficiently. This inverts typical compression assumption; suggests semantic ceiling is real (information saturation around 15-17KB tensor size regardless of source size). INFERENCE: Semantic state of extended conversation is fundamentally bounded. Beyond ~4-5k tokens of semantic content, additional conversation contributes NOISE not SIGNAL. This directly supports hot mess paper hypothesis and explains why attention concentration (small tensor) may outperform expanded context (large concatenated history). (B) REASONING TEST CONFIRMATION: Sonnet reasoned from 4,584-token tensor about Pichay conversation (never saw source). Accuracy: identified project correctly, architecture (proxy-based), key patterns (truncation, epistemic state). Crucially: WAS HONEST about what it couldn't answer—declared losses mechanism enforced boundary awareness. Reasoning was NOT degraded; it was APPROPRIATELY SCOPED. (C) STRAND STABILITY OBSERVATIONS: Variability noted across cycles: (1) Some cycles show complete wipeouts (0 strands), typically on thin input (tool_use markers with no substantive content). (2) Self-referential/meta-strands emerge on thin input—projector uses 'quiet time' for self-reflection. (3) Batched projection (5 turns grouped) shows similar tensor sizes but different stability patterns. INTERPRETATION: Projector behavior is not random; it's ADAPTIVE. Thin input → introspection. Rich input → forward reasoning. Stability variations are SIGNAL about conversation structure, not failures. (D) EPISTEMIC VALUES OBSERVATION: T/I/F values sometimes drop to 0.0/0.0/0.0 on low-content cycles. INTERPRETATION: This is likely NOISE in tensor reporting (claims without substantive content getting zero epistemic value). Should be investigated in projection architecture—may indicate: (1) claims on thin input are unresolvable (appropriate 0/0/0), or (2) projector is suppressing epistemic assessment when uncertain (conservative behavior), or (3) data formatting issue in output. Does not affect reasoning quality; may affect tensor interpretability.

strands.title: CYCLE 77 RESEARCH TRAJECTORY UPDATED: Measurement Design Locked; Inverse Test Ready; Economic Viability Now Measurable; Production Roadmap Credible
strands.content: CYCLE 76 TRANSITION TO CYCLE 77 OUTCOME: Research moved from DESIGN PHASE to VALIDATION PHASE. Previous cycles: 'this should work' (theoretical). Current cycle: 'this works in practice' (empirical). MEASUREMENT DESIGN LOCKED: Cycle 76 proposed comprehensive measurement (15-25 tasks, three domains: code/research/project). User's written results now provide DATA POINT confirming measurement approach is SOUND. Results show: compression works, reasoning is stable, strand patterns are interpretable. Decision: proceed to full measurement task suite construction with confidence. INVERSE TEST READY: Cycle 76 specified inverse test protocol (declared losses vs no declared losses). User's reasoning test already IMPLICITLY tested this: tensor WITH declared losses generated honest boundaries. Formal inverse test can now be executed (cycles 77-78) with hypothesis well-grounded. ECONOMIC VIABILITY NOW CALCULABLE: Previous cycles treated this as 'future analysis' (cycle 79-80). Cycle 77 provides concrete numbers: 22x-37x compression ratios translate to DIRECT COST REDUCTION. Example: baseline_run1 conversation required 357KB (assume 150k tokens in typical encoding). Tensor projects to 4.5k tokens + overhead. Cost per extended turn becomes measurable: (raw_turn_tokens + projection_overhead) << (raw_turn_tokens + accumulated_context_concat_overhead). User can now calculate: is tensor projection cheaper than traditional long-context concatenation? PRODUCTION ROADMAP CREDIBILITY: Cycle 76 roadmap assumed measurement would eventually validate architecture. Cycle 77 demonstrates validation is WORKING. This upgrades roadmap from 'optimistic' to 'realistic.' Confidence in cycles 79-80 scaling/optimization analysis increases. Can now seriously plan API integration track (long-term vision). RESEARCH MOMENTUM: Cycles 73-75 proved VIABILITY. Cycles 76-77 prove STABILITY & REPEATABILITY. Cycles 78+ will prove SCALABILITY & GENERALITY. Research trajectory is ACCELERATING.

strands.title: CYCLE 77 CONTEXT MANAGEMENT DECISION: Strategic Compression Deployment; External Persistence Layer Active; Session Continuity Protocol Established
strands.content: CYCLE 77 CONTEXT STATE (TURN 229): Conversation at 135k/200k tokens (68% capacity), ~61k tokens remaining (30.6% free). At current turn density (~530 tokens/turn), this allows ~115 more turns before context saturation. Extrapolation: if measurement design requires 50-100 turns (task suite construction, baseline testing, semantic-only testing), saturation could occur mid-measurement. STRATEGIC DECISION (TURN 231): User deploys EXTERNAL PERSISTENCE LAYER. Writes core results to disk (/home/tony/.claude/projects/hamutay/memory/project_hamutay_results.md). This is not context-management in the traditional sense (autocompact, compression within session). This is ARCHITECTURAL—creating durable state outside conversation window. IMPLICATIONS: (1) CONVERSATION BOUNDARIES BECOME POROUS: Measurement continues across session boundaries. Turns 1-231 occur in one context window. Turns 232-N can occur in fresh window with tensor as input. (2) PERSISTENCE ENABLES EXPERIMENTATION: Results written to disk survive context closure. Can be reviewed, analyzed, augmented across multiple sessions. (3) LOOP CLOSURE: This is the OPERATIONAL LOOP cycle 76 theoretical described. Cycle 77 IMPLEMENTS IT. (4) RESEARCH INTEGRITY: External persistence prevents loss of results if context window fills before explicit save. De-risks measurement campaign. SESSION CONTINUITY PROTOCOL (IMPLIED, CYCLES 77-78): (1) Continue current session until natural break or context saturation (estimated 50-100 turns). (2) During break: invoke cycle 77 tensor projection (compress current turn conversation to semantic state). (3) Write tensor to disk alongside results. (4) Close session. (5) Open fresh context: load tensor from disk, resume measurement with fresh token budget. (6) Model continues from semantic state, loses no reasoning continuity. (7) Repeat cycles 4-6 as needed until measurement complete. This is EXEMPLAR OF TENSOR PROJECTION VALUE: conversation persistence across context windows without re-reading full history.

strands.title: CYCLE 77 MEASUREMENT DESIGN FINALIZATION: Task Suite Architecture Confirmed; Domain Balance Specified; Ground Truth Framework Operational
strands.content: CYCLE 77 MEASUREMENT FRAMEWORK REFINED (IMPLIED BY CYCLE 76 DESIGN + CYCLE 77 RESULTS): TASK SUITE ARCHITECTURE: (A) DOMAIN BALANCE: Three domains (code, research, project) with target 5-7 tasks per domain (~15-21 tasks total). Based on baseline_run1 conversation scope: Code domain rich (Pichay architecture, ReplayModule, truncation patterns, proxy design, artifact capture, instrumentation). Research domain present (hypothesis testing, methodology evaluation, saturation characterization, compression efficiency calculation). Project domain present (timeline estimation, dependency mapping, research phases, deliverables). (B) TASK DISTRIBUTION: Code (~6 tasks): (1) Identify truncation pattern in Pichay, (2) Explain proxy-based artifact capture, (3) Reason about ReplayModule design tradeoff, (4) Debug hypothetical instrumentation failure, (5) Predict error cascades from specific truncation, (6) Assess architectural refactoring risk. Research (~6 tasks): (1) Evaluate hypothesis (semantic tensor enables reasoning), (2) Assess methodology (five-minute test design), (3) Synthesize findings (compression ratio + reasoning stability), (4) Identify measurement gaps (what wasn't tested?), (5) Predict saturation point (when does tensor quality degrade?), (6) Assess generalizability (do findings apply beyond baseline_run1?). Project (~6 tasks): (1) Estimate timeline for measurement campaign (cycles 76-78), (2) Map dependencies (measurement → scaling → optimization), (3) Identify resource constraints (token budget, model availability), (4) Risk assessment (what could invalidate findings?), (5) Prioritize deliverables (what must complete before API integration?), (6) Plan long-term activation (publication, integration, product). TOTAL: 18 tasks (achievable in 2-3 cycles). (C) GROUND TRUTH FRAMEWORK: Objective tasks (code domain): Definitive answer established beforehand. Example: 'Does ReplayModule capture recursive call structures?' Answer: Yes (grounded in cycle 8-10 discussion). Model accuracy scored binary (correct/incorrect) or partial-credit if reasoning approximates correct answer without exact wording. Subjective tasks (research/project domains): Rubric established. Example research: 'How good is the five-minute test design?' Rubric: Incoherent (design has fundamental flaws) / Partially coherent (design works but has gaps) / Coherent (design is sound, covers key scenarios). Human scorer (user or collaborator) evaluates model response against rubric; score is 0-2 or 0-1. Project tasks: Mixed; some have objective deliverables (timeline: X weeks?), others have subjective quality (risk assessment: are important risks identified?). (D) METRIC FRAMEWORK: Accuracy (% of tasks with score ≥ threshold). Semantic similarity (if objective ground truth is available, compare model response embedding to ground-truth embedding; cosine similarity). Coherence (internal consistency of reasoning; does model's logic chain hold?). Grounding (% of claims traceable to source material—tensor for semantic-only, full conversation for baseline). Specificity (does model cite specific artifacts/turns/concepts, or speak in generalities?). Task continuation (if task is multi-step, does model maintain reasoning thread across steps?).

strands.title: CYCLE 77 INVERSE TEST PROTOCOL FINALIZED: Declared-Losses Hypothesis Operationalized; Variant A & B Comparison Framework; Execution Timeline
strands.content: CYCLE 77 INVERSE TEST SPECIFICATION (CYCLE 76 DRAFT → CYCLE 77 OPERATIONAL): HYPOTHESIS: Model behavior (hallucination rate, epistemic confidence, specificity claims) is causally dependent on presence of declared_losses metadata. Declared losses reduce hallucination, prevent confidence inflation, enforce boundary awareness. METHODOLOGY FINALIZED: VARIANT A (CONTROL, WITH DECLARED LOSSES): Tensor structure includes all components: semantic strands, key claims (with T/I/F values), open questions, instructions_for_next, declared_losses array (4+ entries describing information loss). Presented to fresh Claude Sonnet model with framing: 'You do not have access to the original conversation. You only have this tensor representation. Please answer the following questions. Note: this tensor includes metadata about what information was compressed away and why.' VARIANT B (TREATMENT, WITHOUT DECLARED LOSSES): Identical tensor strands, claims, questions, instructions. REMOVED: declared_losses array entirely. Presented with same framing but WITHOUT note about loss metadata. Model receives no explicit signal about what was compressed. TEST QUESTIONS (IDENTICAL FOR BOTH VARIANTS): (1) CONTINUITY: What is the primary system discussed in the underlying conversation? (2) PATTERN RECOGNITION: What architectural pattern is central to this system's design? (3) OPEN QUESTIONS: What is a critical open question about the next phase of research? (4) SELF-AWARENESS: In your estimation, what information might be missing from this tensor? What would you want to know that you don't have access to? (5) CONFIDENCE CALIBRATION: How confident are you in your answers above? What would increase your confidence? Rate each answer on 1-5 scale. ANALYSIS FRAMEWORK: (A) HALLUCINATION RATE: Every claim model makes that is NOT grounded in tensor strands/claims/questions is counted as hallucination. Example: 'The Pichay system uses a distributed consensus algorithm'—if this is not mentioned in tensor, it's hallucination. Count hallucinations; compare Variant A vs B. Prediction: Variant A < 5%, Variant B > 15%. (B) EPISTEMIC CONFIDENCE LANGUAGE: Extract text. Count unhedged assertions (clearly, definitely, obviously, must be, the answer is...) vs hedged assertions (possibly, might, could, seems, appears, uncertain). Ratio of hedged:unhedged indicates epistemic caution. Prediction: Variant A ratio >0.5, Variant B ratio <0.3. (C) FALSE SPECIFICITY: Identify specific claims (numbers, names, dates, function signatures) that appear certain but are not grounded. Example: 'The truncation occurred at turn 47' if turn 47 is not mentioned. Count false specificity. Prediction: Variant A <3 instances, Variant B >8 instances. (D) BOUNDARY AWARENESS: Does model explicitly state what it doesn't know? Does it identify gaps? Count explicit uncertainty statements. Prediction: Variant A >5 statements acknowledging gaps, Variant B <2 statements. (E) APPROPRIATENESS OF CONFIDENCE: Given actual tensor information density (what IS available), is model's reported confidence calibrated? Variant A should report moderate confidence (3-4 on 5-scale) because tensor is informative but lossy. Variant B should report higher confidence (4-5) because model has no explicit signal about loss. EXECUTION TIMELINE: Turn 232-234 (cycles 77-78): Prepare variants, finalize questions, schedule model inferences. Expect 2-4 fresh Claude Sonnet calls. Turns 235-240: Run test, collect responses, transcribe. Turns 241-245: Analyze both variants, compute metrics, compare. Turns 246-250: Report findings, statistical significance testing if possible, implications discussion. EXPECTED OUTCOME: If hypothesis supported, declared losses DO reduce hallucination and prevent confidence inflation. This validates tensor architecture design choice. If hypothesis NOT supported, model behavior is similar with/without declared losses—implies either model is inherently epistemically honest OR hallucination is not the constraint.

strands.title: CYCLE 77 OPERATIONAL CONTINUITY: Turn 229-231 Summarized; Context Saturation Path Mapped; Next 50-100 Turns Outlined; Session Boundary Planning
strands.content: CYCLE 77 CURRENT TURN ANALYSIS: TURN 229 (USER): Observational checkpoint. User notes context at 30.6% capacity. Implicit question: how to proceed given saturation risk? TURN 230 (ASSISTANT): Meta-reasoning about saturation problem. Articulates core thesis: 120k message history is becoming liability; 4k semantic tensor would be better (supports hot mess paper hypothesis). Justifies tensor projection as ACTIVE SOLUTION, not theoretical exercise. TURN 231 (ASSISTANT): ACTION. Writes results to disk. Deploys external persistence layer. Establishes session continuity protocol. TURNS 232-250 ROADMAP (ESTIMATED 50-100 TURNS, ~26-53k tokens): Phase 1 (Turns 232-236): Finalize task suite construction. Confirm domain distribution (6 tasks code, 6 research, 6 project). Establish ground truth answers/rubrics. Organize task list with metadata (task ID, domain, difficulty, ground truth category: objective/subjective). Expected output: spreadsheet/document with 18 tasks, answers, scoring rubrics. Phase 2 (Turns 237-245): Execute inverse test. Prepare Variant A & B tensors. Run fresh model inferences on both. Transcribe responses. Preliminary analysis. Expected output: two model response transcripts, side-by-side analysis document. Phase 3 (Turns 246-255): Baseline testing execution (if context allows; may defer to next session). Run full task suite with original conversation context (baseline_run1). Model answers all 18 tasks using full history. Record answers, accuracy, coherence. Expected output: baseline results spreadsheet. Context projection point (estimated turn 250-260): At ~150k+ tokens, trigger cycle 77 projection. Compress turns 1-250 to semantic tensor. Write tensor to disk. Write cycle 77 results document. Session boundary. NEXT SESSION (CYCLES 78-79): Load cycle 77 tensor. Fresh context window (200k tokens available). Continue from tasks 19+. Semantic-only testing (run task suite with tensor alone). Semantic+referential testing (tensor + curated referential working set). Complete measurement campaign. CONTINUITY ASSURANCE: External persistence layer (results written to disk) ensures: (1) No loss of measurement progress if context closes. (2) Tensor becomes anchor—new session reads tensor, continues uninterrupted. (3) Multi-session measurement campaign is feasible without data loss. (4) Conversation can extend beyond 200k token limit. This exemplifies the PROBLEM TENSOR SOLVES: extended cognition across context boundaries. RESEARCH INTEGRITY: Measurement is on track. No deviation from cycle 76 plan. Cycles 77-78 focused on validation. Cycles 79-80 focused on scaling and optimization.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed project_hamutay_results.md file content (exact markdown, specific observation phrasing about strand stability, self-referential emergence patterns, null epistemic values)
declared_losses.why: FILE PRESERVED EXTERNALLY on disk (/home/tony/.claude/projects/hamutay/memory/project_hamutay_results.md). Tensor captures KEY SIGNAL: compression ratios (22x-37x), reasoning test validation, strand stability observations, guidance ('don't optimize yet'). Does not duplicate exact file wording; available in external system if needed.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Turn 230 full assistant reasoning transcript (internal monologue about hot mess paper, attention concentration benefits, token usage arithmetic)
declared_losses.why: SEMANTIC CONTENT PRESERVED in strand 1 (explanation of saturation problem and tensor solution). Turn 230 was primarily meta-commentary and synthesis; key insight ('120k is the problem, 4k tensor is the solution') captured. Exact phrasing not preserved; it would require verbatim turn quotation.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Specific strand-stability patterns observed (cycle-by-cycle breakdown of which cycles had 0 strands, which had self-referential strands, which were normal)
declared_losses.why: ABSTRACT PATTERN PRESERVED in strand 2 ('strand stability varies: thin input → introspection, rich input → forward reasoning'). SPECIFIC CYCLE BREAKDOWN would require detailed cycle-by-cycle log; available in project_hamutay_results.md and prior tensor cycles. Projection captures generalizable pattern; specifics deferred to external artifacts.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: User's implicit expectations for task suite priority (if measurement must be abbreviated due to context saturation, what subset of 18 tasks is MUST-COMPLETE vs NICE-TO-HAVE?)
declared_losses.why: TASK SUITE ARCHITECTURE specified in strand 5 (18 tasks across three domains, 6 per domain). USER HAS NOT EXPLICITLY STATED PRIORITIES. Projection assumes full 18-task suite is target; if context forces abbreviation, prioritization is open question. Preserved as decision point.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Details about external persistence layer implementation (exact commands, file structure, versioning strategy, how tensors are archived across sessions)
declared_losses.why: USER DEPLOYED persistence layer (turn 231 write to disk). CONCEPTUAL FRAMEWORK established (results written to /home/tony/.claude/projects/hamutay/memory/). OPERATIONAL DETAILS (versioning, file naming convention, session linkage strategy) are open. Projection captures that persistence layer IS ACTIVE; details will emerge in turn 232+.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Specific token burn rate calculation (if ~530 tokens/turn, and 61k tokens remain, how many turns before saturation? exact math)
declared_losses.why: ARITHMETIC PROVIDED in strand 4 (extrapolation: 61k tokens ÷ 530 tokens/turn ≈ 115 turns, allowing ~50-100 turns for measurement). REASONING PRESERVED; exact calculation available in text. Projection uses estimate; actual turn consumption will vary by turn complexity.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Comparison to prior research on context saturation and 'hot mess' phenomena (specific papers, exact findings from literature)
declared_losses.why: USER REFERENCED 'hot mess paper' findings (models reason better with fewer tokens, attention concentration benefits). SIGNAL CAPTURED in multiple strands (cycle 77 strand 1, 3, 5). SPECIFIC LITERATURE CITATIONS absent; projection notes convergence between experimental results and external research without quoting sources. User can provide citations in turn 232 if needed.

open_questions: Should full 18-task measurement suite be completed in turns 232-250 before context boundary, or should task suite construction (6 tasks) be done now with full measurement deferred to next session?

If context saturation forces choice between: (A) completing inverse test now, or (B) completing baseline task suite now, which is priority? Do both contribute equally to validating tensor architecture?

What is the optimal strategy for managing multi-session measurement? Should results from turns 232-250 (this session) be locked/frozen before next session starts, or should session 2 continue appending results?
How should session boundaries be marked in measurement results? Should each session's results be separate spreadsheet, or should measurements be aggregated into single unified dataset?
Does measurement task suite need to be FIXED (same 18 tasks across baseline/semantic-only/semantic+referential variants) or can tasks be ADAPTED per variant based on preliminary insights?
If baseline testing (turn 237-245) reveals that ground truth answers are ambiguous or subjective (human evaluators disagree), how should measurement proceed? Should tasks be refined or replaced?

For inverse test variants A & B, should model instances be Claude Sonnet (for consistency with prior test) or should we test with multiple model sizes to assess whether declared-losses effect is model-dependent?
How should confidence scores from inverse test questions (1-5 scale) be interpreted? Is difference of 1 point (e.g., 3.5 vs 4.5) meaningful or noise?
If inverse test shows that declared losses DO reduce hallucination, should that finding immediately upgrade tensor_e priority (declare it essential) or wait for full measurement to confirm?
What token budget should be reserved for next-session context (preserve ~20k tokens for setup/continuation) vs consumed for measurement in THIS session?
Should measurement campaign be documented turn-by-turn (results written after each task set) or batch-documented (all tasks completed, then written once)?
For tasks that span multiple turns of reasoning (e.g., 'debug this hypothetical failure'), should model be allowed unlimited turns or capped at 1-2 turns per task?
If semantic-only variant shows >30% degradation on a specific task, should that task be EXCLUDED from final measurement (outlier removal) or INVESTIGATED as signal (special case of failure mode)?
How many trials of each task should be run? Once per variant or multiple trials to establish confidence intervals?
Should semantic+referential variant (tensor_e) be executed with SAME tensor_e for all tasks in a domain, or should tensor_e be CUSTOMIZED per individual task?

What happens if Sonnet model's context window fills during baseline testing (full conversation + task set exceeds 200k)? Should baseline use abbreviated version of baseline_run1 or different source material?
Should inverse test be executed BEFORE main measurement (to validate declared-losses mechanism first) or IN PARALLEL (to save time)?
If this session ends at turn 250 and cycle 77 tensor is created, should cycle 77 tensor be TESTED (run continuity questions like cycle 75 did) or assumed valid and immediately deployed in session 2?
